_meta.note: This is a FlowGraph chart file. Open it with the FlowGraph app to view and edit the interactive dependency flowchart.
_meta.author: Giang Tran
_meta.usage: In the app: click the folder icon (or drag-and-drop this file) to load it. Use Design Mode to edit nodes, groups, and phases.

nodes.name: Digital Transformation Vision
nodes.description: Executive articulation of the target operating model, customer promise, and technology future-state. Sets the North Star for every team, sprint, and architecture decision.
nodes.cinemaScript: Every transformation begins with a question: what does the organisation need to become? The Digital Transformation Vision answers it â€” setting the North Star that every team, every sprint, and every architecture decision will navigate toward. Without a clear vision, modernisation becomes a collection of unconnected projects.

nodes.name: Board Mandate
nodes.description: Formal board resolution authorising the programme. Establishes executive sponsor accountability, strategic priority, and multi-year commitment.

nodes.name: Transformation Roadmap
nodes.description: 18-month rolling plan decomposing the vision into phases, workstreams, milestones, and dependencies. Living document reviewed at each governance cadence.

nodes.name: Budget Allocation
nodes.description: Capital and operational expenditure approved by the Finance Committee. Covers platform, licensing, headcount, change management, and contingency reserve.

nodes.name: Governance Framework
nodes.description: Programme governance model: steering committee cadence, RACI matrix, escalation paths, and decision rights across all workstreams.

nodes.name: Programme Office Setup
nodes.description: PMO stood up with tooling, reporting templates, RAID log, and weekly status rhythm established across all workstream leads.

nodes.name: Change Management Plan
nodes.description: Structured organisational change programme: communication plan, training pathways, champion network, and resistance management playbook.

nodes.name: Stakeholder Alignment
nodes.description: All business unit leaders and key stakeholders aligned on scope, sequencing, and trade-offs. Concerns formally logged and addressed before execution begins.

nodes.name: Risk Framework
nodes.description: Enterprise risk register for the programme. Top risks identified, scored, and assigned owners with mitigation plans and residual risk acceptance.

nodes.name: Vendor Strategy
nodes.description: Cloud hyperscaler selection, SaaS versus build decisions, and strategic partner agreements. Procurement and negotiation approach defined.

nodes.name: Technology Radar
nodes.description: Opinionated inventory of technology choices: adopt, trial, assess, hold. Ensures teams converge on a coherent stack rather than accumulating unconstrained diversity.

nodes.name: Target Architecture Vision
nodes.description: High-level future-state architecture: domain boundaries, platform layers, integration patterns, and key non-functional requirements for the target system.

nodes.name: Data Strategy
nodes.description: Organisation-wide approach to data: ownership model, quality standards, access policy, monetisation potential, and the roadmap from current fragmented state to unified data platform.

nodes.name: Security Strategy
nodes.description: Zero-trust security posture, threat model, compliance obligations, and the migration path from perimeter-based to identity-centric controls.

nodes.name: Programme Kickoff
nodes.description: Full-programme launch event with all workstream leads. OKRs established, accountability assigned, and the first sprint begins.
nodes.cinemaScript: The Programme Kickoff marks the shift from strategy to execution. Budgets are committed, workstreams are resourced, and every team in the organisation is now pointed at the same goal. What happens in the first 90 days from this moment determines whether the transformation gains momentum or stalls under coordination debt.

nodes.name: Enterprise Architecture Blueprint
nodes.description: Comprehensive architecture specification: service topology, integration contracts, data flows, security zones, and deployment patterns. The binding technical contract for all workstreams.

nodes.name: Domain Decomposition
nodes.description: Bounded contexts defined using event storming. Ownership assigned per domain. Explicit upstream/downstream contracts established to minimise cross-team coordination.

nodes.name: API Strategy
nodes.description: API-first design standard: REST, GraphQL, and event-driven contracts. Versioning policy, deprecation process, and developer experience principles.

nodes.name: Event Architecture
nodes.description: Event-driven architecture blueprint: topic taxonomy, schema registry, ordering guarantees, and consumer group patterns across all domains.

nodes.name: Identity & Access Design
nodes.description: Zero-trust IAM blueprint: identity federation, role hierarchy, attribute-based access, service-to-service authentication, and break-glass procedures.

nodes.name: Network Design
nodes.description: VPC topology, transit connectivity, DNS strategy, firewall segmentation model, and hybrid connectivity between on-premises and cloud landing zone.

nodes.name: Observability Strategy
nodes.description: Three-pillar observability: metrics, logs, and traces. SLI/SLO definitions, alerting philosophy, on-call structure, and tooling selection.

nodes.name: DR & Resilience Design
nodes.description: Disaster recovery objectives (RTO/RPO), multi-region failover strategy, chaos engineering mandate, and resilience testing cadence.

nodes.name: Data Architecture
nodes.description: Layered data architecture: landing, raw, curated, and consumption zones. Storage technology selections, partitioning strategies, and SLA tiers.

nodes.name: Microservices Standards
nodes.description: Opinionated service template: API contract shape, health-check endpoints, structured logging, distributed tracing hooks, and CI/CD integration points.

nodes.name: UI/UX Design System
nodes.description: Unified component library, typography, colour tokens, accessibility baseline, and interaction patterns. Shared across all customer-facing and internal surfaces.

nodes.name: Mobile Strategy
nodes.description: Native versus cross-platform decision, feature parity matrix, offline-first requirements, push notification architecture, and mobile CI/CD pipeline design.

nodes.name: Third-Party Integrations Map
nodes.description: Comprehensive inventory of external system integrations: ERPs, payment processors, logistics providers, marketing platforms, and regulatory reporting systems.

nodes.name: Regulatory Compliance Design
nodes.description: Mapping of regulatory obligations (GDPR, PCI-DSS, SOC 2, ISO 27001) to technical controls. Gap analysis against target architecture.

nodes.name: Performance Benchmarks
nodes.description: Quantified performance targets: p95/p99 latency, throughput per service, autoscaling triggers, and degradation thresholds under peak load.

nodes.name: Cost Architecture
nodes.description: FinOps model: tagging taxonomy, team-level cost allocation, reserved capacity strategy, and cloud spend guardrails aligned to approved budget.

nodes.name: Developer Experience Blueprint
nodes.description: Inner-loop developer workflow: local development environment, test-in-isolation patterns, fast feedback loops, and onboarding time targets.

nodes.name: AI/ML Architecture
nodes.description: ML platform blueprint: training infrastructure, feature engineering pipeline, model registry, serving layer, and experiment tracking tooling.

nodes.name: Feature Flags Framework
nodes.description: Feature flag architecture: flag taxonomy, targeting rules, percentage rollout, kill-switch protocol, and flag lifecycle governance.

nodes.name: Architecture Sign-Off
nodes.description: Formal gate: all architecture workstreams reviewed and approved by CTO, security lead, and domain architects. Inconsistencies between data, network, and security blueprints resolved.
nodes.cinemaScript: Eight workstreams arrive at a single gate. The Architecture Sign-Off is not just a quality check â€” it is the binding contract between strategy and delivery. Inconsistencies between network design, data architecture, and security posture caught here cost a sprint to fix; caught in production, they cost months.

nodes.name: Data Discovery & Inventory
nodes.description: Full audit of the existing data estate: sources, ownership, formats, refresh cadences, quality scores, and data classification labels.

nodes.name: Data Quality Assessment
nodes.description: Automated profiling of all source datasets: completeness, uniqueness, timeliness, and referential integrity. Quality baseline established per domain.

nodes.name: Data Governance Policy
nodes.description: Formal data governance: ownership definitions, stewardship model, quality thresholds, lifecycle rules, and cross-border data transfer restrictions.

nodes.name: Master Data Management Design
nodes.description: Golden record strategy for customer, product, and organisational entities. Deduplication rules, survivorship logic, and cross-system synchronisation design.

nodes.name: Data Lineage Framework
nodes.description: End-to-end lineage tracking from source systems through transformations to consumption. Column-level lineage for regulatory reporting and impact analysis.

nodes.name: Data Lake Setup
nodes.description: Cloud object storage provisioned with landing, raw, and curated zones. Lifecycle policies, access controls, and encryption enforced at each tier.

nodes.name: Data Warehouse Migration
nodes.description: On-premises data warehouse migrated to managed cloud DW. Schema translated, historical data loaded, and query performance validated against baseline.

nodes.name: Real-Time Streaming Platform
nodes.description: Managed event streaming platform deployed. Topics, consumer groups, and schema registry configured. End-to-end latency benchmarked under production load.

nodes.name: Customer Data Platform
nodes.description: Unified customer profile assembled from behavioural, transactional, and demographic sources. Identity resolution and consent-gated segmentation enabled.

nodes.name: Data Catalog
nodes.description: Searchable metadata catalogue across the full data estate. Business glossary linked to technical assets, ownership visible, and data quality scores surfaced.

nodes.name: Data Classification
nodes.description: Automated classification of all datasets by sensitivity tier: public, internal, confidential, and restricted. Classification drives access control and encryption policy.

nodes.name: PII Identification & Masking
nodes.description: Automated PII scanner run against all data stores. Masking, tokenisation, and pseudonymisation rules applied per classification tier and regulatory jurisdiction.

nodes.name: Consent Management
nodes.description: Consent capture, storage, and enforcement infrastructure. Data processing agreements mapped to technical controls; preference propagation across all systems.

nodes.name: Analytics Platform
nodes.description: Managed analytics compute provisioned. Notebook environment, SQL query engine, and scheduled job orchestration available to all analytics consumers.

nodes.name: BI Migration
nodes.description: Existing BI reports migrated to the new analytics platform. Data source connections reconfigured; key reports validated for accuracy and performance.

nodes.name: ML Feature Store
nodes.description: Centralised feature store with online and offline serving. Feature definitions versioned, backfilled to training horizon, and shared across model teams.

nodes.name: Data Mesh Implementation
nodes.description: Domain data products published through standardised interfaces. Federated governance applied; each domain responsible for quality, freshness, and SLA compliance.

nodes.name: Data SLAs & Contracts
nodes.description: Formal data SLAs between producing and consuming domains: freshness windows, quality guarantees, schema versioning commitments, and incident response SLOs.

nodes.name: Self-Service Analytics Portal
nodes.description: Business-user-facing portal for ad-hoc queries, dashboard creation, and scheduled report delivery. Row-level security enforced via the data classification policy.

nodes.name: Data Monetisation Strategy
nodes.description: Commercial model for deriving revenue from data assets: insight products, data partnerships, and enrichment licensing. Legal and compliance framework established.

nodes.name: Real-Time Dashboard Platform
nodes.description: Sub-second operational dashboards consuming the streaming platform. Used for live trading, fulfilment monitoring, and customer service escalation.

nodes.name: Data Compliance Certification
nodes.description: Third-party audit confirming GDPR, CCPA, and sector-specific data regulations are met. Evidence package assembled and findings remediated.

nodes.name: Data Access Controls
nodes.description: Attribute-based access control enforced across all data stores. Role-to-data-product mappings certified; privileged access workflows automated.

nodes.name: Historical Data Archive
nodes.description: Long-retention cold storage provisioned. Regulatory retention schedules enforced; retrieval SLAs defined. Data deletion workflows automated for right-to-erasure compliance.

nodes.name: Data Foundation Sign-Off
nodes.description: Formal gate: data mesh, compliance certification, access controls, and SLA contracts all passed. Data foundation ready to serve all downstream application workstreams.

nodes.name: Cloud Landing Zone
nodes.description: Multi-account cloud foundation provisioned via Infrastructure-as-Code. Management hierarchy, guardrails, and baseline security controls active before any workload lands.

nodes.name: IAM Platform
nodes.description: Identity federation between on-premises directory and cloud IdP. Role hierarchies, SCPs, and service-to-service trust policies enforced. Break-glass procedures validated.

nodes.name: Service Mesh
nodes.description: Sidecar proxy mesh deployed across the container platform. mTLS enforced between all services, traffic policies applied, and distributed tracing wired in.

nodes.name: Container Orchestration
nodes.description: Managed Kubernetes clusters provisioned across regions. Node pools, autoscaling policies, network policies, and admission controllers configured.

nodes.name: Secrets Management
nodes.description: Centralised secrets vault deployed. Dynamic secret generation, lease-based expiry, and audit logging active. All hardcoded credentials eliminated.

nodes.name: DNS & Certificate Management
nodes.description: Managed DNS with split-horizon configuration. Automated TLS certificate issuance, rotation, and revocation across all environments.

nodes.name: Artifact Registry
nodes.description: Private container and package registry with vulnerability scanning, retention policies, and promotion gates between environments.

nodes.name: Build Pipeline Standards
nodes.description: Golden pipeline templates: lint, unit test, SAST, container build, publish to registry, and environment promotion. Applied consistently across all service repositories.

nodes.name: GitOps Framework
nodes.description: Declarative GitOps deployment model: environment state stored in Git, reconciliation controllers active, and drift detection alerting configured.

nodes.name: Infrastructure as Code Library
nodes.description: Shared IaC module library for networking, compute, storage, and security patterns. Versioned, tested, and published to the internal registry.

nodes.name: Policy as Code
nodes.description: Compliance policies codified as admission-controller rules. Drift from approved configurations blocked at deploy time, not discovered in audits.

nodes.name: Cost Management Platform
nodes.description: FinOps tooling integrated with all cloud accounts. Team-level cost dashboards live; budget alerts and anomaly detection active.

nodes.name: Developer Portal
nodes.description: Internal developer portal aggregating service catalogue, documentation, API playground, and onboarding guides. Reduces time-to-first-deploy for new engineers.

nodes.name: Internal Documentation Platform
nodes.description: Docs-as-code platform for runbooks, architecture decision records, and API references. Linked from the developer portal and auto-updated by CI pipelines.

nodes.name: Shared Component Library
nodes.description: Published React component library implementing the design system. Consumed by all customer-facing and internal web surfaces. Versioned with a changelog.

nodes.name: API Gateway Platform
nodes.description: Managed API gateway deployed. Routing, rate limiting, request validation, mTLS, and JWT validation active. All external traffic enters through this plane.

nodes.name: Message Bus Platform
nodes.description: Production message bus deployed with DLQ, redelivery policies, and consumer-group isolation. Schema compatibility enforced before publication.

nodes.name: Monitoring & Alerting Platform
nodes.description: Metrics collection, alerting rules, and on-call paging integrated. SLO burn-rate alerts active for all critical services. Runbooks linked from alert definitions.

nodes.name: Log Aggregation Platform
nodes.description: Centralised log pipeline ingesting structured logs from all services. Retention tiers, search indexing, and anomaly detection active.

nodes.name: Distributed Tracing
nodes.description: Trace collection integrated with the service mesh. Cross-service waterfall views, slow-span detection, and dependency topology visible.

nodes.name: Chaos Engineering Platform
nodes.description: Controlled fault injection tooling deployed in staging. Experiment library covering node failure, network partition, latency injection, and resource exhaustion.

nodes.name: Feature Flag Platform
nodes.description: Production-grade feature flag service with percentage rollout, user targeting, kill switches, and audit log. Integrated with the build pipeline via SDK.

nodes.name: A/B Testing Platform
nodes.description: Experimentation infrastructure: experiment assignment service, metric collection pipeline, statistical significance engine, and results dashboard.

nodes.name: CDN & Edge Platform
nodes.description: Global CDN provisioned. Edge caching rules, origin shield, WAF, and DDoS mitigation active. Cache invalidation integrated with the CI/CD pipeline.

nodes.name: Backup & Recovery Platform
nodes.description: Automated backup policies enforced across all stateful services. Recovery tested on a weekly schedule against RTO/RPO targets.

nodes.name: Secrets Rotation Automation
nodes.description: Automated rotation for all long-lived credentials integrated with the secrets vault. Zero-downtime rotation validated for all critical services.

nodes.name: Vulnerability Scanner
nodes.description: Continuous container image scanning, dependency vulnerability detection, and DAST integrated into the build pipeline. Blocker-severity findings break the build.

nodes.name: SIEM Integration
nodes.description: Security events from all platforms forwarded to centralised SIEM. Correlation rules tuned, threat detection alerts active, and SOC integration verified.

nodes.name: Compliance Automation
nodes.description: Continuous compliance monitoring via policy-as-code. Control evidence collected automatically; gap reports generated without manual audit preparation.

nodes.name: Platform Sign-Off
nodes.description: Formal gate: container orchestration, API gateway, message bus, monitoring, and backup platforms all certified ready. Application workstreams cleared to deploy.

nodes.name: Application Portfolio Assessment
nodes.description: Every application in the portfolio evaluated on the 6R spectrum: retire, retain, rehost, re-platform, refactor, or rebuild. Wave sequencing finalised.

nodes.name: Strangler Fig Framework
nodes.description: Shared migration harness for incrementally replacing monolith modules with microservices. Traffic routing, feature-flag integration, and rollback wiring standardised.

nodes.name: Customer Identity Service
nodes.description: Centralised CIAM: registration, login, MFA, session management, and delegated access. Federated with the IAM platform and wired to the consent system.

nodes.name: Product Catalog Service
nodes.description: Domain service managing the full product information model: attributes, pricing hooks, media assets, and category hierarchy. Event-sourced with CDC publishing.

nodes.name: Inventory Management Service
nodes.description: Real-time inventory tracking across warehouses and channels. Reservation, commitment, and availability check APIs integrated with order and fulfilment domains.

nodes.name: Order Management Service
nodes.description: Order lifecycle service: create, allocate, fulfil, return. Saga-pattern orchestration across payment, inventory, and fulfilment domains with compensating transactions.

nodes.name: Payment Processing Service
nodes.description: PCI-DSS compliant payment service: authorisation, capture, refund, and dispute workflows. Idempotency enforced; fraud checks integrated before authorisation.
nodes.cinemaScript: Payments is the most scrutinised service in the entire platform â€” it carries regulatory liability, fraud risk, and the direct trust of every customer. Its dependencies on Identity, the Order chain, and the fraud detection layer make it the convergence point of three separate risk dimensions simultaneously.

nodes.name: Pricing Engine
nodes.description: Dynamic pricing service: rule-based and ML-assisted price computation across channels, customer segments, and promotional overlays.

nodes.name: Promotion & Discount Service
nodes.description: Promotion lifecycle management: creation, validation, stacking rules, and expiry. Coupon redemption, basket-level discounts, and promotional budget guardrails.

nodes.name: Loyalty Programme Service
nodes.description: Points accrual, tier management, redemption workflows, and partner reward integrations. Customer-facing programme rules synchronised with the pricing engine.

nodes.name: Customer Profile Service
nodes.description: Unified customer record aggregating identity, preferences, order history, loyalty status, and consent flags. Read-optimised with event-driven updates.

nodes.name: Notification Service
nodes.description: Multi-channel notification dispatch: email, SMS, push, and in-app. Template management, delivery tracking, and unsubscribe mechanics enforced per channel.

nodes.name: Communication Preferences
nodes.description: Customer-managed channel, frequency, and content preferences. Propagated to the notification service and respected by all outbound communications.

nodes.name: Search Service
nodes.description: Managed search engine indexed with products, content, and customer-facing catalogue. Query relevance tuned, filters faceted, and personalisation signals integrated.

nodes.name: Recommendation Engine
nodes.description: Collaborative filtering and content-based recommendation models served via the feature store. A/B test framework wired in for algorithm comparison.

nodes.name: Content Management System
nodes.description: Headless CMS for marketing content, landing pages, and editorial. Preview environments, localisation hooks, and CDN cache invalidation integrated.

nodes.name: Customer Web Portal
nodes.description: Primary customer-facing web application assembled from CMS content, search, product catalogue, and user profile. Deployed to edge with SSR/SSG hybrid strategy.

nodes.name: Mobile App (iOS)
nodes.description: Native iOS application delivered on the mobile strategy blueprint. Feature-flag gated rollout, biometric auth, and offline-first data sync.

nodes.name: Mobile App (Android)
nodes.description: Native Android application at feature parity with iOS. Dual delivery pipeline, Play Store release management, and crash analytics integrated.

nodes.name: Admin Operations Portal
nodes.description: Internal operations dashboard: order management, inventory overrides, customer service actions, and real-time monitoring widgets. Role-based access enforced.

nodes.name: Seller Portal
nodes.description: Marketplace seller onboarding, product listing, pricing, inventory, and settlement portal. Wired to the product catalogue and order management domains.

nodes.name: Customer Service Tooling
nodes.description: Agent desktop integrating customer profile, order history, communication log, and case management. Escalation workflows and SLA tracking built in.

nodes.name: Fraud Detection Service
nodes.description: Real-time transaction scoring using rule-based and ML models. SIEM-integrated for pattern correlation; results injected into the payment authorisation flow.

nodes.name: KYC / Compliance Service
nodes.description: Know-Your-Customer identity verification workflows. Third-party verification provider integrated, decisioning rules configurable, and audit trail immutable.

nodes.name: Reporting Suite
nodes.description: Finance, operations, and executive reporting built on the data platform. Scheduled delivery, drill-down capability, and data lineage exposed to report consumers.

nodes.name: Legacy Decommission Plan
nodes.description: Formal shutdown sequence for legacy systems: dependency freeze dates, data migration completion criteria, hardware disposition, and licence reclamation schedule.

nodes.name: CRM Migration
nodes.description: CRM data migrated to the new customer profile and order management domains. Bi-directional sync maintained during cutover window. Legacy CRM sunset on completion.

nodes.name: ERP Integration Layer
nodes.description: Adapter layer connecting the ERP to the new domain services: finance events, inventory signals, and procurement triggers routed through the message bus.

nodes.name: Legacy API Decommission
nodes.description: Traffic migrated from legacy API endpoints to new services. Consumers identified and migrated; legacy endpoints deprecated on confirmed zero-traffic date.

nodes.name: Data Migration for Applications
nodes.description: Application-level data migrated to new schemas on the cloud data platform. Validation scripts confirm row counts, referential integrity, and business rule compliance.

nodes.name: User Journey Redesign
nodes.description: Customer journeys redesigned using the new capabilities: personalisation, real-time inventory, and frictionless checkout. Validated with usability testing.

nodes.name: Accessibility Compliance
nodes.description: WCAG 2.1 AA compliance achieved across all customer-facing surfaces. Automated and manual audits completed; remediation tracked to closure.

nodes.name: Localisation Platform
nodes.description: i18n infrastructure: translation management, locale-aware formatting, and RTL layout support. Localisation pipeline integrated with the CMS and build process.

nodes.name: Performance Optimisation Pass
nodes.description: Systematic optimisation of the customer portal and core APIs against the performance benchmarks: bundle splitting, query plan tuning, and cache strategy review.

nodes.name: Security Hardening Pass
nodes.description: Comprehensive security hardening: container image minimisation, RBAC tightening, dependency vulnerability remediation, and secrets rotation verified across all services.

nodes.name: Dependency Upgrades
nodes.description: All service dependencies updated to supported versions. Breaking changes resolved; automated dependency update pipeline activated for ongoing maintenance.

nodes.name: Tech Debt Retirement Phase 1
nodes.description: Highest-priority technical debt items retired: deprecated patterns removed, shared modules extracted, and test coverage raised to baseline thresholds.

nodes.name: Feature Parity Validation
nodes.description: New platform validated against the full feature inventory of legacy systems. Each capability mapped and confirmed in the new system or formally de-scoped.

nodes.name: App Modernisation Sign-Off
nodes.description: Formal gate: CRM migration, data migration, accessibility, performance, security hardening, and feature parity all confirmed. Application workstream cleared for integration.

nodes.name: Zero-Downtime Migration Playbook
nodes.description: Runbook for production cutover: traffic shifting sequence, health-check gates, automatic rollback triggers, and war-room communication protocol.

nodes.name: External API Catalogue
nodes.description: Published catalogue of all public and partner-facing APIs with OpenAPI specs, authentication guides, rate limits, and SLA commitments.

nodes.name: Partner API Programme
nodes.description: B2B API partnership onboarding: developer portal access, sandbox provisioning, commercial terms, and tiered rate limit agreements for strategic partners.

nodes.name: Third-Party Payment Gateway
nodes.description: Integration with primary and fallback payment gateways: tokenisation handshake, 3DS2 support, settlement reconciliation, and PCI-DSS scope containment.

nodes.name: Logistics Provider Integration
nodes.description: Real-time shipping rate quoting, label generation, and tracking event ingestion wired to the order management domain via the API gateway.

nodes.name: ERP Sync Service
nodes.description: Bi-directional ERP synchronisation for financial postings, purchase orders, and inventory adjustments. Reconciliation jobs and exception queues configured.

nodes.name: CRM Sync Service
nodes.description: Outbound sync of customer profile updates and interaction events to the legacy CRM during the dual-run cutover window. Sunset on CRM decommission.

nodes.name: Analytics Events Pipeline
nodes.description: Clickstream and behavioural events collected from all surfaces, validated against schemas, and routed to the analytics platform and data lake.

nodes.name: Marketing Automation Integration
nodes.description: Customer segments from the CDP pushed to the marketing automation platform. Campaign triggers wired to loyalty, promotion, and recommendation events.

nodes.name: Customer Support Integration
nodes.description: Ticketing and live-chat platform integrated with the customer service tooling. Case routing, escalation triggers, and SLA clock synchronised.

nodes.name: Fraud Network Integration
nodes.description: Industry fraud network feeds integrated with the fraud detection service. Shared blacklists, consortium velocity signals, and device fingerprint enrichment.

nodes.name: Regulatory Reporting Integration
nodes.description: Automated feeds from financial and compliance services to regulatory reporting portals. Submission schedules, rejection handling, and audit trails configured.

nodes.name: B2B EDI Integration
nodes.description: EDI 850/856/810 transaction sets integrated for enterprise wholesale customers. AS2 transport, functional acknowledgements, and exception management active.

nodes.name: Webhook Platform
nodes.description: Outbound webhook delivery service: event subscriptions, retry with exponential backoff, delivery receipts, and consumer debug tooling.

nodes.name: GraphQL Federation Layer
nodes.description: Federated GraphQL schema stitching domain subgraphs. Supergraph published via the API gateway for web and mobile consumers.

nodes.name: Rate Limiting & Throttling
nodes.description: Token-bucket rate limiting enforced at the API gateway per consumer, endpoint, and method. Adaptive throttling active for DDoS mitigation.

nodes.name: API Security Controls
nodes.description: OWASP API Top 10 controls enforced at the gateway: input validation, injection prevention, and automated schema conformance checks in the CI pipeline.

nodes.name: Integration Test Harness
nodes.description: Shared test harness for end-to-end integration scenarios across domain boundaries. Contracts verified; data isolation enforced via test-scoped sandboxes.

nodes.name: Contract Testing Framework
nodes.description: Consumer-driven contract tests for all internal and external API boundaries. Pact broker integrated into all CI pipelines; breaking changes blocked on publish.

nodes.name: API Versioning Strategy
nodes.description: Semantic versioning policy enforced across all APIs. Deprecation timeline, sunset notifications, and compatibility matrix published to the developer portal.

nodes.name: Developer Portal APIs
nodes.description: API catalogue, try-it console, and OAuth application registration exposed through the developer portal. Self-service onboarding enabled for external partners.

nodes.name: Sandbox / Test Environment APIs
nodes.description: Fully isolated sandbox environment with synthetic data and configurable response stubs. Available to internal teams and external partners under NDA.

nodes.name: API Analytics
nodes.description: Usage metrics, latency percentiles, and error rates per consumer tracked in the analytics platform. Capacity planning and partner billing powered by this data.

nodes.name: SLA Monitoring for APIs
nodes.description: Automated SLA compliance dashboard tracking availability, latency, and error rate against committed thresholds per partner and consumer tier.

nodes.name: Integration Runbooks
nodes.description: Operational runbooks for all external integrations: failover procedures, escalation contacts, circuit-breaker thresholds, and data reconciliation scripts.

nodes.name: Integration Sign-Off
nodes.description: Formal gate: external API catalogue, rate limiting, API security, and contract testing all passing. All third-party integrations validated end-to-end.

nodes.name: Test Strategy & Pyramid
nodes.description: Formal test strategy: unit, integration, contract, E2E, performance, chaos, and compliance test tiers defined with coverage targets and CI/CD placement.

nodes.name: End-to-End Test Suite
nodes.description: Full user journey test suite covering checkout, account management, fulfilment, and support flows. Executed against a production-like staging environment.

nodes.name: Performance & Load Testing
nodes.description: Load, stress, and soak tests validating p95 latency and throughput against peak-day profiles. Autoscaling behaviour and database connection pooling verified.

nodes.name: Security Penetration Testing
nodes.description: External penetration test of all public surfaces and internal lateral-movement scenarios. Critical findings must be remediated before go-live approval.

nodes.name: Accessibility Testing
nodes.description: Automated axe-core scans and manual screen-reader testing across all customer-facing surfaces. WCAG 2.1 AA pass confirmed.

nodes.name: Chaos & Resilience Testing
nodes.description: Controlled fault injection experiments covering zone failure, dependency timeout, and data store unavailability. All services meet their defined SLO targets under fault.

nodes.name: Data Quality Testing
nodes.description: Automated data quality checks run against migrated datasets. Row counts, null rates, and business rule violations compared to source-system baselines.

nodes.name: Regression Suite Automation
nodes.description: Full regression suite parallelised and integrated into CI. Flaky tests quarantined; pass rate above threshold before merge to main branches.

nodes.name: User Acceptance Testing
nodes.description: Business stakeholders validate the transformed system against defined acceptance criteria. Showstopper defects require resolution before go-live approval.

nodes.name: Compliance Audit
nodes.description: Third-party audit against SOC 2, ISO 27001, PCI-DSS, and GDPR. Evidence package assembled from automated compliance tooling; gaps risk-accepted or remediated.

nodes.name: Third-Party Security Audit
nodes.description: Independent security firm validates fraud detection, payments, and identity services. Findings triaged; critical and high severity issues closed before go-live.

nodes.name: Production Readiness Reviews
nodes.description: PRR checklist completed for all critical services: SLO defined, alerting active, runbook linked, on-call trained, and canary strategy documented.

nodes.name: Disaster Recovery Drills
nodes.description: Full DR rehearsal: primary region failover triggered, RTO and RPO measured, and runbook gaps closed. Results signed off by the business continuity team.

nodes.name: Incident Runbook Validation
nodes.description: All production incident runbooks dry-run by on-call teams. Response time targets measured; tooling access, escalation contacts, and rollback procedures confirmed.

nodes.name: Synthetic Monitoring Setup
nodes.description: Synthetic user-journey checks running in production and staging. Failures page on-call immediately; results feed into the SLO burn-rate dashboard.

nodes.name: Canary Deployment Validation
nodes.description: Canary deployment pattern validated end-to-end: 1% traffic slice, automated metric gates, and automatic rollback on SLO breach confirmed in staging.

nodes.name: Rollback Playbook Validation
nodes.description: Zero-downtime rollback procedures validated for all critical services. GitOps revert, database rollback, and feature-flag kill switch tested under time pressure.

nodes.name: Customer Beta Testing
nodes.description: Closed beta with a representative customer cohort. Feedback captured, critical issues resolved, and net promoter score baseline established before full launch.

nodes.name: Launch Readiness Review
nodes.description: Cross-functional go/no-go review: all PRRs passed, DR drills complete, incident runbooks validated, and customer beta feedback addressed. Cleared for go-live approval.

nodes.name: Go-Live Approval Gate
nodes.description: Final executive go/no-go. Compliance audit, third-party security audit, launch readiness, and rollback validation all passed. Executive sponsor signs cutover authority.
nodes.cinemaScript: Nine months of work, eight hundred decisions, and twenty-five teams converge on a single gate. The Go-Live Approval requires that functional correctness, performance under peak load, regulatory compliance, and disaster recovery readiness all pass simultaneously. Any one failure resets the launch clock.

nodes.name: Phased Traffic Rollout
nodes.description: Traffic progressively shifted from legacy to new platform using weighted DNS and feature flags: 1% â†’ 10% â†’ 50% â†’ 100% with automated metric gates at each step.

nodes.name: Feature Flag Controlled Launch
nodes.description: New features enabled progressively via feature flags. Each flag activated in sequence as monitoring confirms stability. Kill switches armed throughout.

nodes.name: Customer Communication Launch
nodes.description: Customer-facing launch communications: email, in-app, and social announcements timed to traffic milestones. Support team briefed and surge-staffed.

nodes.name: Operations Handover
nodes.description: Formal handover from programme delivery to steady-state operations. On-call rotas transferred, escalation paths updated, and SRE team assumes ownership.

nodes.name: SRE On-Call Runbooks
nodes.description: Production SRE runbooks activated in the ops wiki. First on-call rotation under new ownership completes without critical incident.

nodes.name: Post-Launch Monitoring
nodes.description: Enhanced monitoring posture for the first 30 days: 24/7 war-room coverage, lowered alert thresholds, and daily SLO review. Anomalies triaged within 15 minutes.

nodes.name: Legacy System Cutover
nodes.description: Legacy system traffic reduced to zero following the phased rollout. DNS cut, load balancer rules removed, and legacy application servers stopped.

nodes.name: Legacy Decommission Execution
nodes.description: On-premises servers shut down per the decommission plan. Hardware returned to lessor, data destruction certificates issued, and decommission confirmed in the asset register.

nodes.name: Licence Reclamation
nodes.description: Software licences from decommissioned legacy systems returned or terminated. Savings realised against the original TCO model.

nodes.name: Post-Launch Performance Review
nodes.description: 30-day performance review: p95/p99 latency actuals versus targets, throughput peaks, and database query performance compared to pre-launch benchmarks.

nodes.name: Customer Feedback Collection
nodes.description: Structured feedback collected via in-app surveys, NPS pulse, and customer interviews. Themes categorised and fed into the product backlog.

nodes.name: NPS & CSAT Measurement
nodes.description: Net Promoter Score and Customer Satisfaction metrics baseline established for the new platform. Compared to pre-transformation benchmarks and programme KPIs.

nodes.name: Defect Triage & Resolution
nodes.description: Post-launch defect queue triaged by severity. P1 defects remediated within SLA; P2/P3 batched into fortnightly release cycles.

nodes.name: Hotfix Pipeline Activation
nodes.description: Emergency hotfix pipeline validated and on-call teams trained. First live hotfix deployed within the 4-hour RTO without affecting in-flight transactions.

nodes.name: Security Incident Response Drill
nodes.description: Tabletop and live security incident simulation. SIEM alerts triggered, incident commander engaged, and containment playbook executed within the target response time.

nodes.name: Cost Optimisation Review
nodes.description: 30-day spend analysis against the FinOps model. Oversized instances right-sized, idle resources terminated, and Reserved Instance coverage expanded.

nodes.name: Observability Tuning
nodes.description: Alert thresholds recalibrated post-launch. False positives silenced, missing coverage gaps filled, and distributed trace sampling rates tuned for cost versus fidelity.

nodes.name: Capacity Planning Update
nodes.description: Resource forecasts updated using 30-day production traffic patterns. Reserved capacity adjusted and autoscaling policies tuned for the observed demand curve.

nodes.name: AI/ML Model Monitoring
nodes.description: Recommendation and fraud models monitored for data drift, prediction quality degradation, and business metric impact. Retraining triggers defined.

nodes.name: Data Quality Monitoring
nodes.description: Automated data quality checks running continuously in production. SLA breaches alert data stewards; lineage impacts surfaced automatically.

nodes.name: Continuous Improvement Backlog
nodes.description: Post-launch improvement themes collected from teams, customers, and monitoring data. Backlog groomed and prioritised for the first steady-state sprint cycles.

nodes.name: Lessons Learned Workshop
nodes.description: Programme-level retrospective: what went well, what failed, and systemic improvements for the next transformation. Output feeds the organisation's delivery playbook.

nodes.name: Programme Closure Report
nodes.description: Formal programme closure: KPIs measured against the original business case, savings realised, and lessons learned published to the executive leadership team.

nodes.name: Next Phase Roadmap
nodes.description: 18-month roadmap for the next modernisation wave, informed by lessons learned, NPS feedback, and platform capabilities unlocked during this transformation.

nodes.name: Digital Transformation Complete
nodes.description: Programme formally closed. The organisation operates on a fully modern, cloud-native, data-driven platform. The transformation is complete â€” the next evolution has already begun.
nodes.cinemaScript: The transformation is complete â€” but the real transformation is in the organisation that built it. Distributed ownership, automated delivery, observable systems, and data-driven decisions are now the default. The question is no longer whether the company can modernise, but how fast it can move.

groups.description: Landing zone, IAM, service mesh, container orchestration, secrets, and policy â€” the security and compute substrate

groups.description: Artifact registry, build pipelines, GitOps, API gateway, message bus, monitoring, logging, and tracing

groups.name: Commerce Services
groups.description: Product catalogue, inventory, orders, payments, pricing, promotions, and loyalty â€” the transactional heart of the platform

groups.description: Customer profile, communication preferences, search, CMS, web portal, mobile apps, localisation, and journey redesign

groups.description: All external and internal integrations: partner APIs, payment gateways, logistics, ERP, CRM, marketing, fraud networks, and API governance

phases.name: Strategy
phases.description: Establish the vision, board mandate, governance, budget, and programme office before any technical work begins

phases.name: Architecture
phases.description: Design the full enterprise architecture: service topology, data architecture, security, network, observability, and developer experience

phases.name: Data Foundation
phases.description: Build the unified data platform: lake, warehouse, streaming, governance, classification, consent, and compliance certification

phases.name: Platform
phases.description: Provision the full cloud platform: landing zone, IAM, containers, service mesh, CI/CD, observability, security controls, and shared services

phases.name: Modernisation

phases.name: Integration
phases.description: Connect all external systems: partner APIs, payment gateways, logistics, ERP, marketing automation, fraud networks, and API governance

phases.name: Validation

phases.name: Operations
phases.description: Phased rollout, legacy cutover, decommission, post-launch monitoring, cost optimisation, NPS measurement, and programme closure
